name: Minor Hall
abstract: Minor Hall (March 2, 1897 – October 16, 1959), better known as Ram Hall, was an American jazz drummer active on the New Orleans jazz scene. He was the younger brother of Tubby Hall.

concerts.label: Kid Ory and His Creole Jazz Band

works.t: Bill Bailey, Won't You Please Come Home?
I'm Busy and You Can't Come In

about_quotes.speaker: Ed Garland
about_quotes.evidence: I'll start with Buster Wilson on the piano. Joe Dorersbourg on clarinet. Papa Mutt [Carey] on the trumpet. Ram Hall on the drums. ... During that time, who did I have in that band. Lloyd Glenn, Joe, ah, Ram, myself, Buster Wilson

about_quotes.speaker: William Russell
about_quotes.evidence: That was the greatest rhythm section I ever heard, I'd say, was that Kid Ori rhythm section with, who's the bass player? Eddie Garland and Bud Scott and Minor Hall

about_quotes.speaker: Don Ewell
about_quotes.evidence: Oh, yeah, Ed Garland. Yes. Oh, I had a lot of enjoyment playing with that section. Ram Hall. Yeah. Played a lot of... With a lot of flavor.

about_quotes.speaker: Ed Garland
about_quotes.evidence: We had Minor Hall on drums... and myself on bass, and King Oliver was playing. That's the way the band was picked up when we went to Frisco

about_quotes.speaker: Buddy Burns and Frank Pasley
about_quotes.evidence: Ed Garland on the bass, Minor Hall on the drums, Ory, and myself. I played up there with them for a long time.
about_quotes.doc_title: Buddy Burns and Frank Pasley Interview, 1968-09-23

about_quotes.speaker: Andrew Blakeney
about_quotes.evidence: The personnel of that movie was myself, Kid Ory, Buster Wilson, Montudie Garland, Barney Bigard and Ram Hall.
